free_text: Fission fragment mass yields and total kinetic energy
release in neutron-induced fission of 233U from
800 MeV protons beam onto an unmoderated
cylindrical ionization chamber with two back-to-back
Both volumes used shared cathode between them and a
separate circular anode with Frisch grid.
Samples on carbon foil were secured to the cathode by
Signal from the cathode was used for timing, two
signals from the anodes were used to determine energy
Signals from the Frisch grid were used to determine
90L WNR flight path with 11.9 m length.
The pulsed beam structure consisted of a series of
125 ps micropulses spaced 1.8 mu-s apart and grouped
into 675 us macropulses delivered at 40 Hz.
For production of low energy neutrons in 1L target,
the flight path was 22 m and macropulse repetition
Energy of fission fragments was determined from
- momentum transfer of the incident neutron, and
TKE of post neutron emission fragments
(72 ug/cm2 thick) on carbon foil backing
